title: verify work taxonomy verify
purpose: APP-A-verify defines work taxonomy verify for the agent-driven expert system. Human job taxonomy → pack workflows.
scope: Owns `APP-A-verify` only; siblings under `—` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §3 — Branch A — Pursuit & control plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#3-branch-a-pursuit-control-plane).
behavior_steps: Pack workflows bind each verify slice to explicit unit, integration, E2E, tool-operator, or conformance commands so [G2.1](G2.1-goal-verify-command-state-pack.md) goal_verify can aggregate them mechanically—never subjective "looks good" gates.

When `evidence_required` is true, the conductor spawns Verifier or tool-operator workers via [G1.1](G1.1-task-verify-router-verifier.md) and verify-router.py with the task card's literal Test or Tool command block rather than improvising checks.

Shell workers write logs under `evidence/` per docs/operator/evidence-types.md and return paths; the conductor dual-writes `evidence_files` and sets `last_verify` to passed before advancing `next_action` or marking implement tasks complete.

Conformance scripts ([G3.1](G3.1-conformance-validate-workflow-ci.md), route-tier checks) run at pursuit boundaries so goal.state cannot move from pursuing to achieved without aggregated proof across named commands.

On verify failure, missing evidence, or goal_verify aggregation gaps, pursuit fails closed at H2—[G1.3](G1.3-last-verify-before-advance.md) blocks advance and [B3.3](B3.3-escalation-loop-on-verify-fail.md) may escalate without clearing gates.

**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.

acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: APP-A-verify defines work taxonomy verify for the agent-driven expert system. Human job taxonomy → pack workflows. In practice, the system pack workflows bind each verify slice to explicit unit, integration, e2e, tool-operator, or conformance commands so [g2.1](g2.1-goal-verify-command-state-pack.md) goal_verify can aggregate them mechanically—never subjective "looks good" gates, then when `evidence_required` is true, the conductor spawns verifier or tool-operator workers via [g1.1](g1.1-task-verify-router-verifier.md) and verify-router.py with the task card's literal test or tool command block rather than improvising checks.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent meta 2026-06-28 -->

Verify work spans unit, integration, E2E, tool outputs, goal_verify, and conformance scripts. Verification is not a phase humans optionally trigger—it is the gate that separates pursuing from achieved.

Pack workflows must name verify commands explicitly so goal_verify can aggregate them.
